description: Using placeholder text as the only label for a form control, leaving it with no accessible name once the user types.
body: Failure mode: a field relies on placeholder text instead of a real label, so the accessible name is empty (or vanishes on input) and screen-reader users cannot tell what the field is for. Detection signals: axe reports a control with no accessible name; the placeholder disappears when typing; no <label> or aria-label in the markup. Do not treat placeholder as a substitute for a label. Safer fallback: add a programmatic label via <label for> or aria-labelledby, keep the placeholder only as a hint, and verify a non-empty name in the accessibility tree.
